subject: Geography
title: Cave Diving
description: A quick, practical guide to Cave Diving.

hook.text: Cave diving is one of the most dangerous sports on Earth—zero visibility, no air, no exit above.

buildup.text: Divers carry multiple tanks, reels of guide line, and backup lights into flooded passages.

discovery.text: They've explored underwater caves over 10 km long and 300 meters deep.

twist.text: Silt stirred by a single fin kick can reduce visibility to zero in seconds.

climax.text: Every new passage mapped is a place no human—and often no light—has ever reached.

punchline.text: The bravest explorers on Earth go where there's no surface and no light.

quiz.question: What makes cave diving especially dangerous?
options: No direct access to the surface and zero visibility risks
